description: The PROP-007 save-failure test at note_body_editor_handlers.rs:93-132 uses a non-deterministic failure path (/root/should-fail-permission.md). The test branches on whether the write succeeded or failed (lines 120-128: 'if write_result.is_err() { ... } else { ... }'), weakening the failure-path assertion. If the test is run as root, the write to /root/ succeeds, and the test silently validates the success path instead of the failure path it claims to test. A test designed to verify 'isDirty preserved on failed save' must consistently exercise the failure path, not have a built-in escape hatch.
expected: Use a reliable failure mechanism, such as: (a) write to a path inside a directory with permissions 000, (b) use a mock/trait-based filesystem abstraction, (c) set the target path to a read-only filesystem path that is guaranteed to fail regardless of user. The test should assert exactly one outcome (failure) and fail hard if the precondition isn't met.
